)

results.question: List owners whose total spending is above the average spending of owners in their own city, without using GROUP BY in the outer query.
results.query_family_reason: per-entity aggregate total then ranked/compared (derived relation)
results.sql:
WITH owner_spending AS (
    SELECT 
        o.owner_id,
        o.owner_name,
        o.city,
        SUM(p.total_amount) AS total_spent
    FROM owners o
    JOIN purchases p ON o.owner_id = p.owner_id
    GROUP BY o.owner_id, o.owner_name, o.city
),
city_averages AS (
    SELECT 
        city,
        AVG(total_spent) AS avg_spent
    FROM owner_spending
    GROUP BY city
)
SELECT 
    os.owner_id,
    os.owner_name,
    os.city
FROM owner_spending os
JOIN city_averages ca ON os.city = ca.city
WHERE os.total_spent > ca.avg_spent
